items.subject: Shareholder Dilution from $40B ATM Offering
items.quote: the Company may offer and sell, from time to time in its sole discretion, up to $40 billion of shares of Class A Common Stock and Class C Capital Stock
items.reason: The evidence quote describes Alphabet's own ATM offering program, meaning Alphabet IS the issuer (the Company doing the selling), not an entity threatened by dilution — the relationship direction implies an external threat to Alphabet, but the quote shows Alphabet itself initiating the offering, and moreover the quote does not use any language of threat or harm.

items.subject: Alphabet $40B At-the-Market Equity Offering Program

items.subject: Alphabet Appoints New Principal Accounting Officer
items.quote: On June 5, 2026, Alphabet Inc. ( Alphabet ) announced the appointment of Marsida Saraci, Vice President and Controller, as its Principal Accounting Officer, effective immediately.
items.reason: The relationship direction is inverted: the appointment announcement is a catalyst event *for* or *by* Alphabet Inc., but the claim states the announcement is a catalyst_for Alphabet Inc. itself, which is semantically unclear or backwards — the correct framing would be that Alphabet Inc. is the subject that made the appointment, not that the announcement caused Alphabet Inc. to exist or act.

items.subject: Amazon.com, Inc.
items.object: Amazon $17.5B Delayed Draw Term Loan Facility
items.quote: Amazon.com, Inc. (the Company ), Citibank N.A., as administrative agent, and the lenders party thereto entered into a term loan agreement (the DDTL Credit Agreement ). The DDTL Credit Agreement provides the Company with a $17.5 billion senior unsecured delayed draw term loan credit facility
items.reason: The relationship 'produces' is incorrect; Amazon.com, Inc. is the borrower/recipient of the credit facility, not its producer — the facility was created by the lenders and Citibank N.A. as administrative agent through a term loan agreement.
